detailed_guidelines: The central focus is on the practical aspects of patient access and the structure of service delivery. This matters because these regulations determine the availability and effectiveness of healthcare for various populations.
detailedDescription: The central focus is on the practical aspects of patient access and the structure of service delivery. This matters because these regulations determine the availability and effectiveness of healthcare for various populations.

detailed_guidelines: This theme encompasses regulations governing care provided after an acute hospital stay, including in Long-Term Care Hospitals (LTCHs), Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities (IRFs), and Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs). Key issues include the three-day prior hospitalization rule for SNF admission, the "60% Rule" for IRF classification, and site-neutral payment policies impacting LTCHs. This is distinct from general nursing home operations, focusing on the transitional care continuum. This matters because PAC is critical for patient recovery and managing healthcare system costs.
detailedDescription: This theme encompasses regulations governing care provided after an acute hospital stay, including in Long-Term Care Hospitals (LTCHs), Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities (IRFs), and Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs). Key issues include the three-day prior hospitalization rule for SNF admission, the "60% Rule" for IRF classification, and site-neutral payment policies impacting LTCHs. This is distinct from general nursing home operations, focusing on the transitional care continuum. This matters because PAC is critical for patient recovery and managing healthcare system costs.

detailed_guidelines: This theme consolidates recommendations and concerns unique to rural health settings. It includes requests for exemptions or flexibilities for rural providers from regulations like price transparency and new Conditions of Participation for obstetric services. It also covers specific issues for Critical Access Hospitals (CAHs) and Rural Health Clinics (RHCs) regarding billing, reporting, and statutory requirements like the CAH 96-hour length of stay rule. This matters because rural communities face significant access-to-care challenges that are exacerbated by one-size-fits-all regulations.
detailedDescription: This theme consolidates recommendations and concerns unique to rural health settings. It includes requests for exemptions or flexibilities for rural providers from regulations like price transparency and new Conditions of Participation for obstetric services. It also covers specific issues for Critical Access Hospitals (CAHs) and Rural Health Clinics (RHCs) regarding billing, reporting, and statutory requirements like the CAH 96-hour length of stay rule. This matters because rural communities face significant access-to-care challenges that are exacerbated by one-size-fits-all regulations.

description: Mental and Behavioral Health Services Access. This theme addresses systemic barriers to accessing mental and behavioral health care. || It includes concerns about provider shortages, inadequate insurance networks, the Institutions for Mental Disease (IMD) exclusion in Medicaid, and the 190-day lifetime limit for inpatient psychiatric care under Medicare
detailed_guidelines: It also covers the specific challenges of finding providers willing to diagnose and treat adult ADHD. This is critical given the recognized national crisis in mental health access.
detailedDescription: It also covers the specific challenges of finding providers willing to diagnose and treat adult ADHD. This is critical given the recognized national crisis in mental health access.

description: Preventive Care and Wellness Services. This theme covers regulations related to services aimed at preventing disease and promoting health. || It includes restrictive Medicare coverage for Medical Nutrition Therapy (MNT), Diabetes Self-Management Training (DSMT), and Intensive Behavioral Therapy (IBT) for obesity
detailed_guidelines: The core argument is that the system is misaligned, focusing on "sick care" rather than prevention, and that payment and coverage policies should be reformed to support preventive interventions
detailedDescription: The core argument is that the system is misaligned, focusing on "sick care" rather than prevention, and that payment and coverage policies should be reformed to support preventive interventions

description: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
detailed_guidelines: This theme is dedicated to the regulatory framework for PACE organizations. It includes concerns that current rules create "state-sanctioned monopolies" by limiting service area overlap, impose redundant audits, and have slow application and enrollment processes (e.g., no mid-month enrollment). The core issue is that regulations are seen as outdated and are actively hindering the expansion of this high-quality, cost-effective model of care.
